Pool: Mooring Allocation - Additional Marina Moorings
Comments:
Under the Shoreline Plan adopted by the TRPA Governing Board on October 24, 2018, and per TRPA Code of Ordinances Subsection 84.3.2(E)(2), TRPA may permit a maximum of 330 additional marina moorings. Per TRPA Code of Ordinances Subsection 84.3.2(E)(2)(a), additional moorings shall be allocated for use by marinas as either buoys or boat slips.
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
On August 28, 2024, the TRPA Governing Board approved the Meeks Bay Restoration Project. As a mitigation measure for the project, 119 moorings were returned to the Marina pool to mitigate the loss of mooring capacity and launching capacity from the permanent closure of the Meeks Bay Marina. These moorings were to be prioritized for west shore marinas (Camp Richardson, Obexers, Homewood, Sunnyside, Tahoe City Marina), but may be used for other areas as directed by the Shoreline Steering Committee.

Pool: Mooring Allocation - Additional Private Moorings
Comments:
Under the Shoreline Plan adopted by the TRPA Governing Board on October 24, 2018, and per TRPA Code of Ordinances Subsection 84.3.2(E)(2), TRPA may permit a maximum of 1,486 additional private moorings. 

Per TRPA Code of Ordinances Subsection 84.3.2(E)(2)(a), additional moorings shall be used for private moorings as either buoys or boat lifts.

Pool: Mooring Allocation - Additional Public Agency Moorings
Comments:
Under the Shoreline Plan adopted by the TRPA Governing Board on October 24, 2018, and per TRPA Code of Ordinances Subsection 84.3.2(E)(2) TRPA may permit a maximum of 300 additional public agency moorings. 
Per TRPA Code of Ordinances Subsection 84.3.2(E)(2)(a), additional moorings shall be allocated for use by public agencies for use as buoys or boat slips.

Persons-at-one-time (PAOT) - Summer Day Use Reserved Pool (Marina)
Comments: Per the Marina Master Plan Guidelines, accessory marina uses do not require PAOTS as they normally cater to persons already using a marina and do not represent additional service capacity. However, certain uses would draw in and serve more people so they are not considered accessory and do require PAOTs. Jet ski rentals are one example that would require summer day use PAOTs. PAOTs for these non-accessory uses should not be drawn from the marina reserve PAOT pool.
